---
title: "Predicting Home Prices in Ames, Iowa"
subtitle: "An End-to-End Machine Learning Project"
author: "Neža Podpeskar"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    center: true
    controls: true
    progress: true
    css: styles.css
---

## Predicting Home Prices in Ames, Iowa

An End-to-End Machine Learning Project

Neža Podpeskar

Regression + Classification + Streamlit App

---

## The Problem

Home pricing is one of the biggest financial decisions people make — yet pricing is often subjective and inconsistent.

**Can we use data to answer two questions?**

- What will a home sell for?
- Is a home "premium" (top 25% by price)?

---

## The Data

- Ames Housing dataset, Iowa (2006-2010)
- 2,927 home sales after cleaning
- 90 features: size, quality, garage, neighborhood, and more

---

## Our Approach

- Cleaned and explored the data
- Engineered new features from raw columns
- Split data into training and test sets, carefully, to avoid leakage
- Trained several models, from simple to advanced
- Evaluated everything honestly on unseen test data

---

## What We Found

- Typical price prediction error: ~$12,000
- Premium-home classification accuracy: 96%
- Strongest predictors: overall quality, size, neighborhood

---

## Complex Isn't Always Better

Simple linear and logistic models performed almost as well as advanced XGBoost models.

This matters. In real-world use, a model that's easy to explain and trust is often just as valuable as a slightly more accurate one.

---

## The Final Application

A live web app where anyone can:

- Get an instant price prediction
- Check if a home is "premium"
- Explore neighborhood insights

Try it: ames-housing-by-nezky.streamlit.app

---

## Key Takeaways

- Machine learning can meaningfully support pricing decisions
- Quality, size, and location matter most
- Simpler models are worth considering, not just complex ones
- AI-assisted tools still require careful human verification

---

## Thank You

Questions?
